name: spec-workflow
description: 仕様駆動開発（SDD）ワークフローを自動発動で強制。Subtask開始時にAC確認、TDD実行、完了時にACチェックを実施。スコープ外実装を禁止。Use when implementing features, creating new functionality, or when "実装して", "作成して", "開発して" keywords appear.
capabilities: Automatically activates when implementation keywords are detected
Validates acceptance criteria before starting implementation
Verifies all AC requirements before task completion
Manages scope to prevent feature creep
Integrates with branch and PR skills automatically
author: Clionegohan
securitySummary: This skill contains ONLY documentation and workflow guidance in Markdown format. No executable code, scripts, or network calls exist. All static findings are FALSE POSITIVES: code examples are illustrative pseudocode in Markdown fences, not shell commands; hash identifiers flagged as cryptographic algorithms are content metadata; example dialogues flagged as C2 keywords are user assistance examples; path references are legitimate documentation links. The skill is a pure prompt-based workflow enforcer.
valueStatement: Development teams struggle to maintain consistent methodology across features. This skill automates TDD enforcement, acceptance criteria validation, and scope management for every implementation task.
limitations: Requires pre-defined Subtask files with acceptance criteria
Does not create specifications (use /spec skill instead)
Only supports projects following EPIC-Story-Subtask structure
Workflow is enforced for each task individually

useCases.title: Maintain development discipline
useCases.description: Follow TDD methodology consistently for every feature implementation

useCases.title: Standardize workflow
useCases.description: Enforce acceptance criteria and scope boundaries across team members

useCases.title: Quality gate enforcement
useCases.description: Ensure all implementations meet AC requirements before PR creation

promptTemplates.title: Start subtask
promptTemplates.prompt: 実装して/spec-workflow：specs/{epic-id}/{story-id}/{subtask-id}.mdを読んでACを確認。ユーザーに「このACで進めますか？」と聞いてからTDDサイクルを開始して。

promptTemplates.title: Resume work
promptTemplates.prompt: spec-workflowで継続：現在のSubtaskの進捗を確認して。次にACのどの項目から再開すべきか建議して。

promptTemplates.title: Scope check
promptTemplates.prompt: spec-workflow：依頼された機能が現在のSubtaskのACに含まれているか確認。スコープ外なら提案のみにして。

promptTemplates.title: Complete task
promptTemplates.prompt: spec-workflowで完了処理：AC全項目の達成状況を確認。テストが全て通過していることを確認してから/subprでPR作成を提案して。

bestPractices: Break down requirements into clear acceptance criteria before implementation
Write failing tests first, then implement minimum code to pass
Verify each acceptance criterion is satisfied before completing
antiPatterns: Implementing features not defined in acceptance criteria

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code platforms.

faq.question: How does it activate?
faq.answer: Automatically triggers when implementation keywords like 実装して or create are used.

faq.question: What happens if AC is unclear?
faq.answer: Skill prompts you to clarify ambiguous acceptance criteria before proceeding.

faq.question: Is my data secure?
faq.answer: Prompt-based skill only. No file access, network calls, or code execution.

faq.question: How does it integrate with other skills?
faq.answer: Automatically calls /branch for branches and /pr for pull requests during workflow.

faq.question: How is this different from /spec?
faq.answer: /spec handles upstream specification drafting. This skill handles downstream implementation following those specs.
